---
title: "codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0 — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [5, 8, 5, 15, 15, 16, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-10-vs-day-0 codon Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control) and columns are ribosome sites (A-site / P-site / E-site, left to right). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against log2(odds ratio) on the x-axis (x range about -2.5 to +2.5); each glyph is one codon, coloured by the physicochemical class of its encoded residue (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple, stop = grey). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is log2(OR) = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.5, and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/composite/codon_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one codon at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of ~62 (61 sense codons plus the in-frame stop TGA); within a panel a glyph to the right of zero is enriched at day_10 relative to day_0 within that condition, to the left is depleted, and higher up the panel means a smaller FDR. The two rows let the same (site, codon) cell be read in BWM and control side by side.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv` (aa-level parent of this contrast)
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), within-condition day_10-vs-day_0 contrast per (condition, site, codon); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of ~62 codons
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, codon) 2x2 day-10-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of ~62 codons (372 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 reps per (condition,timepoint) summed before Fisher. Same anti-conservative concern as per-timepoint Fisher.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are whole-transcriptome; tiny effects yield tiny p-values; odds_ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is per (condition,site) within each contrast file (~20 aa or ~61 codon tests per family).
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition and between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, these tests do NOT mix conditions or timepoints; each Fisher is held within one (condition,contrast) cell. Structurally cleaner on the pooling-as-confound axis; positive design feature.

---

## Headline

At codon-level within-condition Fisher day_10 vs day_0, 330/372 tests clear FDR<0.05 (BWM 162/186, control 168/186): 148 site x codon cells significant in both conditions, 14 BWM-only, 20 control-only. Minimum adjusted p underflows to 0.0 (7 cells) at whole-transcriptome pooled N (totals 1.3M-3.4M); the informative axis is `effect change`, not p.

The in-frame stop codon TGA carries the largest swings in the file (A:TGA BWM `log2_OR`=+1.039 vs control -1.235; P:TGA BWM +1.744; E:TGA BWM +2.145) but rests on small counts in at least one arm and is flagged `low-count` / unstable — it should not anchor the read. Among stable sense codons, the largest BWM-vs-control divergences concentrate at site A: A:CCC (BWM -0.542 / control +0.264), A:TGG (the sole Trp codon; BWM -0.586 / control +0.088, mirroring the AA file's A:W split), A:CGG (BWM -0.031 / control +0.566), A:GGC (BWM -0.359 / control +0.188), A:CTG (BWM -0.115 / control +0.404). At equal billing, the largest shared-direction sense cells (both arms same sign) are E:CCA both depleted (BWM -0.538 / control -0.242), A:CCA both depleted (-0.249 / -0.148), P:CGG both enriched (+0.402 / +0.488), P:CCG both enriched (+0.334 / +0.368), A:GAT both enriched (+0.306 / +0.051).

## Top hits

`log2_OR` is the within-condition Fisher effect for the day_10 vs day_0 contrast: positive = enriched at day_10 relative to day_0 within that condition, negative = depleted. Each row pairs the BWM and control value for one (site, codon) cell; `effect change` = BWM `log2_OR` - control `log2_OR`. A value near zero means both conditions moved together from day_0 to day_10 (a shared trajectory); a large-magnitude value means the two conditions' day_10-vs-day_0 trajectories diverge (positive when BWM rose relative to control, negative when control rose relative to BWM). The three sections split every cell significant at FDR<0.05 in at least one condition by which condition(s) reach significance: both, BWM only, control only. Within each section rows are grouped by `site` in A -> P -> E order, then sorted by `effect change` descending (no row cap); cells significant in neither condition are omitted. Because the pooled per-condition totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts, almost every shown cell clears FDR<0.05 and p magnitude is uninformative. The cells that matter most are therefore those with the largest `|effect change|` (the divergences at the top and bottom of each site block), not those with the smallest p. The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon; the `flag` column carries `low-count`, which in this file fires only on the in-frame stop codon TGA.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in both conditions (n = 148 site x codon cells)

| site | codon | aa | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | +1.039 | -1.235 | +2.274 | low-count (C) |
| A | AAC | N | +0.171 | -0.235 | +0.406 |  |
| A | GTT | V | +0.301 | -0.050 | +0.351 |  |
| A | AAT | N | +0.305 | -0.035 | +0.340 |  |
| A | GTC | V | +0.182 | -0.138 | +0.319 |  |
| A | ACC | T | +0.053 | -0.250 | +0.303 |  |
| A | GAT | D | +0.306 | +0.051 | +0.255 |  |
| A | ATC | I | +0.149 | -0.090 | +0.238 |  |
| A | GAG | E | +0.178 | +0.016 | +0.161 |  |
| A | GAC | D | +0.060 | -0.090 | +0.150 |  |
| A | GCC | A | -0.055 | -0.180 | +0.125 |  |
| A | ATG | M | +0.207 | +0.096 | +0.111 |  |
| A | AAG | K | -0.125 | -0.228 | +0.103 |  |
| A | CGT | R | +0.056 | -0.029 | +0.084 |  |
| A | CAA | Q | -0.160 | -0.237 | +0.077 |  |
| A | AGT | S | +0.231 | +0.172 | +0.059 |  |
| A | TCA | S | +0.150 | +0.110 | +0.040 |  |
| A | AGC | S | +0.088 | +0.051 | +0.036 |  |
| A | TGT | C | +0.231 | +0.202 | +0.030 |  |
| A | GGA | G | -0.062 | -0.089 | +0.027 |  |
| A | TTT | F | +0.154 | +0.139 | +0.015 |  |
| A | TTC | F | +0.080 | +0.070 | +0.010 |  |
| A | GCT | A | +0.028 | +0.023 | +0.006 |  |
| A | TCC | S | -0.098 | -0.095 | -0.003 |  |
| A | CTC | L | -0.144 | -0.137 | -0.007 |  |
| A | GGT | G | -0.159 | -0.125 | -0.034 |  |
| A | CTT | L | -0.129 | -0.094 | -0.036 |  |
| A | CAC | H | -0.216 | -0.175 | -0.041 |  |
| A | GAA | E | +0.120 | +0.164 | -0.044 |  |
| A | CCA | P | -0.249 | -0.148 | -0.101 |  |
| A | TAT | Y | -0.034 | +0.088 | -0.122 |  |
| A | ATA | I | +0.040 | +0.185 | -0.145 |  |
| A | TCG | S | +0.087 | +0.243 | -0.156 |  |
| A | GGG | G | +0.106 | +0.266 | -0.160 |  |
| A | ACA | T | -0.062 | +0.121 | -0.182 |  |
| A | TTA | L | -0.054 | +0.163 | -0.217 |  |
| A | GTG | V | -0.303 | +0.047 | -0.350 |  |
| A | TTG | L | -0.287 | +0.079 | -0.366 |  |
| A | CGA | R | -0.054 | +0.341 | -0.395 |  |
| A | CCG | P | -0.038 | +0.362 | -0.400 |  |
| A | TAC | Y | -0.447 | -0.043 | -0.404 |  |
| A | TGC | C | -0.280 | +0.131 | -0.412 |  |
| A | CTA | L | -0.180 | +0.253 | -0.432 |  |
| A | GCA | A | -0.273 | +0.165 | -0.438 |  |
| A | CCT | P | -0.173 | +0.271 | -0.444 |  |
| A | GCG | A | -0.133 | +0.346 | -0.479 |  |
| A | CTG | L | -0.115 | +0.404 | -0.519 |  |
| A | GGC | G | -0.359 | +0.188 | -0.547 |  |
| A | TGG | W | -0.586 | +0.088 | -0.674 |  |
| A | CCC | P | -0.542 | +0.264 | -0.806 |  |
| P | TTA | L | +0.388 | -0.074 | +0.462 |  |
| P | CTC | L | +0.109 | -0.152 | +0.261 |  |
| P | TTG | L | +0.140 | -0.109 | +0.249 |  |
| P | TCC | S | +0.062 | -0.154 | +0.216 |  |
| P | ATA | I | +0.278 | +0.077 | +0.201 |  |
| P | GTC | V | +0.029 | -0.169 | +0.198 |  |
| P | GAG | E | +0.277 | +0.092 | +0.185 |  |
| P | ATG | M | +0.103 | -0.066 | +0.170 |  |
| P | ACC | T | -0.056 | -0.218 | +0.161 |  |
| P | TCG | S | +0.294 | +0.154 | +0.140 |  |
| P | GCC | A | -0.058 | -0.179 | +0.121 |  |
| P | CTT | L | -0.037 | -0.150 | +0.113 |  |
| P | CTG | L | +0.206 | +0.099 | +0.107 |  |
| P | ATC | I | -0.121 | -0.222 | +0.101 |  |
| P | ACG | T | +0.341 | +0.243 | +0.098 |  |
| P | CCC | P | +0.267 | +0.191 | +0.076 |  |
| P | AAG | K | -0.232 | -0.296 | +0.065 |  |
| P | CAC | H | -0.113 | -0.162 | +0.049 |  |
| P | GTG | V | +0.137 | +0.095 | +0.043 |  |
| P | CCA | P | -0.195 | -0.234 | +0.039 |  |
| P | TCA | S | +0.102 | +0.068 | +0.034 |  |
| P | TAC | Y | -0.100 | -0.124 | +0.024 |  |
| P | AAA | K | +0.046 | +0.023 | +0.022 |  |
| P | CAA | Q | -0.091 | -0.113 | +0.022 |  |
| P | GAA | E | +0.123 | +0.102 | +0.020 |  |
| P | GTT | V | -0.049 | -0.050 | +0.001 |  |
| P | CAG | Q | +0.092 | +0.096 | -0.004 |  |
| P | ACA | T | +0.075 | +0.087 | -0.013 |  |
| P | ATT | I | -0.090 | -0.075 | -0.014 |  |
| P | GCG | A | +0.286 | +0.314 | -0.028 |  |
| P | CGT | R | -0.062 | -0.033 | -0.029 |  |
| P | CCG | P | +0.334 | +0.368 | -0.033 |  |
| P | AGA | R | -0.136 | -0.079 | -0.057 |  |
| P | AAC | N | -0.133 | -0.073 | -0.061 |  |
| P | GAC | D | -0.042 | +0.027 | -0.069 |  |
| P | GGG | G | +0.146 | +0.218 | -0.071 |  |
| P | TTT | F | +0.099 | +0.174 | -0.075 |  |
| P | GCT | A | -0.046 | +0.035 | -0.081 |  |
| P | CCT | P | +0.276 | +0.357 | -0.081 |  |
| P | CGG | R | +0.402 | +0.488 | -0.086 |  |
| P | GGC | G | +0.063 | +0.159 | -0.096 |  |
| P | TGT | C | +0.197 | +0.296 | -0.099 |  |
| P | TAT | Y | -0.035 | +0.066 | -0.101 |  |
| P | AGG | R | +0.252 | +0.372 | -0.120 |  |
| P | AAT | N | +0.085 | +0.206 | -0.120 |  |
| P | AGT | S | +0.063 | +0.226 | -0.164 |  |
| P | GGA | G | -0.274 | -0.109 | -0.165 |  |
| P | GAT | D | +0.103 | +0.297 | -0.194 |  |
| P | GGT | G | -0.173 | +0.063 | -0.235 |  |
| P | CGA | R | -0.037 | +0.410 | -0.446 |  |
| E | TCC | S | +0.153 | -0.053 | +0.207 |  |
| E | TCG | S | +0.414 | +0.254 | +0.160 |  |
| E | TCT | S | +0.202 | +0.047 | +0.156 |  |
| E | CAT | H | +0.182 | +0.035 | +0.147 |  |
| E | CAC | H | +0.059 | -0.075 | +0.134 |  |
| E | GTC | V | +0.043 | -0.070 | +0.114 |  |
| E | TCA | S | +0.241 | +0.145 | +0.096 |  |
| E | TAC | Y | -0.058 | -0.148 | +0.090 |  |
| E | GAG | E | +0.045 | -0.041 | +0.087 |  |
| E | CAA | Q | +0.045 | -0.038 | +0.082 |  |
| E | ATG | M | +0.134 | +0.053 | +0.082 |  |
| E | ACC | T | -0.110 | -0.186 | +0.076 |  |
| E | GGA | G | -0.137 | -0.208 | +0.071 |  |
| E | TTA | L | +0.160 | +0.092 | +0.068 |  |
| E | GCC | A | -0.070 | -0.131 | +0.061 |  |
| E | CAG | Q | +0.133 | +0.077 | +0.056 |  |
| E | ACA | T | +0.189 | +0.136 | +0.053 |  |
| E | CTC | L | +0.123 | +0.072 | +0.051 |  |
| E | ACG | T | +0.346 | +0.299 | +0.046 |  |
| E | GGC | G | +0.230 | +0.188 | +0.043 |  |
| E | GGG | G | +0.258 | +0.218 | +0.040 |  |
| E | CGT | R | -0.065 | -0.100 | +0.035 |  |
| E | AAC | N | -0.070 | -0.090 | +0.020 |  |
| E | AGC | S | +0.136 | +0.118 | +0.018 |  |
| E | GAT | D | +0.096 | +0.083 | +0.013 |  |
| E | ATC | I | -0.073 | -0.083 | +0.011 |  |
| E | AGT | S | +0.248 | +0.252 | -0.004 |  |
| E | TTC | F | -0.031 | -0.024 | -0.007 |  |
| E | AAT | N | +0.104 | +0.114 | -0.009 |  |
| E | TGT | C | +0.112 | +0.122 | -0.010 |  |
| E | AAG | K | -0.186 | -0.171 | -0.014 |  |
| E | GAA | E | +0.090 | +0.125 | -0.034 |  |
| E | CTA | L | +0.195 | +0.238 | -0.044 |  |
| E | AGA | R | -0.163 | -0.116 | -0.047 |  |
| E | ATA | I | +0.191 | +0.239 | -0.048 |  |
| E | GTT | V | -0.094 | -0.042 | -0.052 |  |
| E | GCG | A | +0.239 | +0.305 | -0.066 |  |
| E | TTT | F | +0.034 | +0.107 | -0.073 |  |
| E | CGA | R | +0.271 | +0.373 | -0.102 |  |
| E | ACT | T | -0.081 | +0.030 | -0.111 |  |
| E | GTG | V | -0.029 | +0.100 | -0.130 |  |
| E | AAA | K | -0.032 | +0.102 | -0.134 |  |
| E | CTG | L | +0.153 | +0.291 | -0.138 |  |
| E | GTA | V | -0.082 | +0.080 | -0.163 |  |
| E | CGG | R | +0.300 | +0.496 | -0.196 |  |
| E | CCG | P | +0.054 | +0.301 | -0.247 |  |
| E | CCA | P | -0.538 | -0.242 | -0.296 |  |
| E | CCT | P | -0.128 | +0.281 | -0.409 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in BWM only (n = 14 site x codon cells)

| site | codon | aa | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | ATT | I | +0.295 | +0.005 | +0.290 |  |
| A | ACT | T | +0.043 | -0.015 | +0.057 |  |
| A | CGC | R | -0.033 | +0.000 | -0.034 |  |
| A | GTA | V | -0.168 | -0.015 | -0.153 |  |
| P | TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | +1.744 | -0.375 | +2.119 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| P | TTC | F | -0.037 | -0.015 | -0.021 |  |
| E | TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | +2.145 | -0.633 | +2.777 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| E | TTG | L | +0.138 | +0.006 | +0.132 |  |
| E | TGC | C | +0.106 | -0.015 | +0.120 |  |
| E | TGG | W | +0.083 | -0.001 | +0.084 |  |
| E | GCT | A | -0.046 | -0.014 | -0.032 |  |
| E | TAT | Y | -0.039 | +0.001 | -0.040 |  |
| E | CTT | L | -0.034 | +0.013 | -0.047 |  |
| E | ATT | I | -0.096 | -0.013 | -0.083 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in control only (n = 20 site x codon cells)

| site | codon | aa | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | AGA | R | +0.022 | -0.064 | +0.086 |  |
| A | CAG | Q | +0.014 | -0.058 | +0.073 |  |
| A | AAA | K | -0.002 | +0.019 | -0.021 |  |
| A | TCT | S | -0.023 | +0.042 | -0.065 |  |
| A | CAT | H | +0.003 | +0.075 | -0.072 |  |
| A | ACG | T | +0.030 | +0.251 | -0.222 |  |
| A | AGG | R | +0.004 | +0.398 | -0.394 |  |
| A | CGG | R | -0.031 | +0.566 | -0.597 |  |
| P | TGG | W | +0.035 | -0.107 | +0.141 |  |
| P | CGC | R | +0.029 | -0.042 | +0.071 |  |
| P | TCT | S | -0.023 | -0.032 | +0.009 |  |
| P | CAT | H | +0.022 | +0.047 | -0.026 |  |
| P | GTA | V | +0.031 | +0.059 | -0.028 |  |
| P | ACT | T | -0.008 | +0.058 | -0.066 |  |
| P | GCA | A | -0.017 | +0.180 | -0.197 |  |
| P | CTA | L | -0.041 | +0.171 | -0.212 |  |
| E | CGC | R | +0.023 | -0.090 | +0.113 |  |
| E | GCA | A | +0.021 | +0.138 | -0.117 |  |
| E | CCC | P | +0.018 | +0.215 | -0.197 |  |
| E | AGG | R | -0.015 | +0.244 | -0.258 |  |

### Flag glossary

- `low-count` — at least one condition arm's day-10 / day-0 footprint count for this codon is below the stability threshold (BWM < 50 / control < 50); the parenthetical names which arm(s) tripped it, where `BWM` = BWM and `C` = control (so `(BWM, C)` means both arms are low and `(C)` only control). In this file the flag fires only on the in-frame stop codon TGA. `log2_OR` (and therefore `effect change`) is unstable at small counts, so a large `|effect change|` for a flagged row may reflect sampling noise rather than biology.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 372 (186 per condition; 3 sites x ~62 codons each)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 330 (BWM 162/186, control 168/186)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 337
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow; 7 cells at exactly 0.0). Smallest non-zero p_adj is 3.434934e-251 (P, BWM, GAG).
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the millions has no meaningful floor; the dominant concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide).
- Per (condition, site) sig at FDR<0.05: BWM A 54/62, P 52/62, E 56/62; control A 58/62, P 58/62, E 52/62.
- Section split: 148 cells sig in both, 14 BWM-only, 20 control-only.

## Biological interpretation

**Near-universal FDR significance is a large-N artifact; the readable axis is `effect change`, not p (concrete).** 330 of 372 cells clear FDR<0.05 and 7 underflow to exactly 0.0, with pooled per-condition totals of 1.3M-3.4M footprints per arm. At that N Fisher's exact returns a vanishing p for a trivially small composition shift, so "significant in both / BWM-only / control-only" is a weak sorter and p magnitude carries no information. What this file is actually built to read is how differently the two conditions moved from day_0 to day_10 at each (site, codon) cell, captured by `effect change` = BWM `log2_OR` - control `log2_OR`: near zero is a shared trajectory, large magnitude is a divergence.

**The largest BWM-vs-control divergences concentrate at the A site and are opposite in direction, not just magnitude (concrete).** At the decoding (A) site, control enriches a codon over the timecourse while BWM depletes it or stays flat: A:CCC (BWM -0.542 / control +0.264, the section's largest divergence at `effect change` -0.806), A:TGG (the sole Trp codon; BWM -0.586 / control +0.088), A:CGG (control-only, BWM -0.031 / control +0.566), A:GGC (BWM -0.359 / control +0.188), A:CTG (BWM -0.115 / control +0.404). Because TGG is Trp's only codon, the aa sister's A:W direction split localises here with no synonym ambiguity, and the proline and arginine divergences (CCC, CGG) point at A-site decoding of those residues changing in opposite directions between the perturbed and unperturbed lines across the 10-day window. These are firm FDR cells, but per the large-N caveat the direction and magnitude carry the signal, not the p.

**Shared-direction cells mark a day_10-vs-day_0 trajectory that both conditions follow (concrete).** The cells with the largest magnitude but near-zero `effect change` move the same way in both arms: E:CCA both depleted (BWM -0.538 / control -0.242), A:CCA both depleted (-0.249 / -0.148), P:CGG both enriched (+0.402 / +0.488), P:CCG both enriched (+0.334 / +0.368), A:GAT both enriched (+0.306 / +0.051). The proline (CCA, CCG) and arginine (CGG) codons that recur here have the canonical A/P-site peptidyl-transfer (proline, eIF5A/EF-P-dependent) and exit-tunnel electrostatic (basic-residue) anchors, so a coordinated day_10 shift is mechanistically plausible; but because both conditions share it, it more likely reflects a baseline compositional drift over the timecourse than a condition-specific perturbation, which is exactly what the within-condition binomial baseline can adjudicate.

**The TGA stop spike is low-count instability, not a readthrough finding (concrete).** The three largest `effect change` cells in the entire file are the in-frame stop codon TGA: E:TGA +2.777, A:TGA +2.274, P:TGA +2.119, all carrying the `low-count` flag because at least one arm sits below 50 counts. TGA is an in-frame stop (likely selenocysteine recoding or readthrough) and its odds ratio is dominated by the smallest cell, so the magnitude is statistically real but biologically unstable across replicates. Quote its direction at most, never its magnitude, and never let it anchor the read.

**Whether an amino-acid-level divergence concentrates on one synonym is the question codon resolution adds (exploratory).** For Trp the A-site split is trivially carried by TGG; for multi-codon residues the picture is informative — at the A site proline's CCC diverges hard (`effect change` -0.806) while its synonym CCA barely moves (-0.101, shared depletion), so the divergence is synonym-specific rather than spread across all proline codons. That pattern is a codon-usage signature (a tRNA-isoacceptor or transcriptome-composition shift), but at whole-transcriptome N it is a magnitude ranking rather than a clean hit, so it stays exploratory until it reproduces. The natural cross-checks are the aa sister (`aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0`, does the divergence survive aggregation to the amino acid?), the other two contrasts (`*_d10_vs_d5` and `*_d5_vs_d0`, is the trajectory monotonic or biphasic, and does d10_vs_d0 ~= d5_vs_d0 + d10_vs_d5 per cell?), and `codon_within_condition_binomial` (is the cell a stable baseline both conditions carry, or genuinely group-variable?).

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the A-site BWM-vs-control divergence where control enriches and BWM depletes (A:CCC, A:TGG, A:CGG, A:GGC, A:CTG) is the file's design-target reading; the shared proline/arginine day_10 trajectory (E:CCA, P:CGG, P:CCG) is real but shared. *Exploratory*: synonym-specific concentration of an aa-level divergence (CCC vs CCA at the A site) — test it in the aa sister, the other two within-condition contrasts, and the binomial baseline. *Noise*: the in-frame stop TGA spike (low-count, flagged), the raw p magnitudes, and the near-universal significance count, all of which are large-N or small-count artifacts rather than per-codon biology.

## Methods

Fisher's exact (two-sided) on a 2x2 of (codon_count, total - codon_count) at day_10 vs day_0 within each condition. `global_codon_occ_stats.py` (Analysis 3b) pools the replicate counts per (condition, timepoint) before the test and applies BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of ~62 codons (corrected by `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`); each of the 6 (condition, site) sub-families is corrected independently, not across the full 372-row file. Effect is reported as `log2_OR` (log2 of the CSV `odds_ratio`): >0 = enriched at day_10 relative to day_0 within that condition, <0 = depleted. The test does not compare BWM against control directly (that is the `control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction` reading carried by the paired columns) and does not test enrichment vs the transcriptomic background (the `within_condition_binomial` family). About 62 of 64 codons appear per (condition, site); the in-frame stop TGA is present, TAA/TAG are not retained in the significant set.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The 2x2 contingencies pool the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) before the test, so per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic and p-values run anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` / `effect change` as the effect, not a tiny p as extra certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled per-condition totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (day_10 ~1.56M BWM / ~1.26M control; day_0 ~2.11M BWM / ~3.37M control), so Fisher returns vanishing p (7 cells underflow to 0.0) for trivially small deviations — rank by effect, never by p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is applied within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of ~62 codons, not across the 372-test file, so two cells with the same raw p in different families can land at different `p_adj` — here significance is carried by section membership, not by a displayed p.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file cleanly answers "does day_10 differ from day_0 within this condition", leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to the side-by-side paired columns (the `effect change`), not to the test itself.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** The design-target reading: sense codons whose two conditions split direction over the timecourse, concentrated at site A (A:CCC, A:TGG, A:CGG, A:GGC, A:CTG) — read these from the paired BWM/control columns, not from either arm alone.
- **near-universal-sig-large-N.** 330/372 cells clear FDR<0.05, so which section a cell lands in (both / BWM-only / control-only) is weakly discriminating — rank by `|effect change|` within and across sections, not by section membership or p.
- **rare-codon-low-count.** The `low-count` flag (BWM < 50 / control < 50) fires only on the in-frame stop TGA (A:TGA, P:TGA, E:TGA); every sense codon clears the threshold, so for the TGA rows quote direction, not the extreme magnitude.
- **stop-codon-instability.** TGA carries the file's three largest effect changes (E:TGA +2.777, A:TGA +2.274, P:TGA +2.119) on small counts in at least one arm — treat as low-support instability, not a sense-codon signal; it marks the same cells as `rare-codon-low-count`.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM A-site codon volcano (x = log2(odds ratio), about -2.5 to +2.5; y = -log10(FDR); glyphs coloured by encoded-residue class, triangles = FDR<0.05). The proline codon CCC (neutral orange) and the Trp codon TGG (hydrophobic green) sit at the far left (~-0.5 to -0.6) pinned at the top of the panel as the largest depletions; the in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the lone far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+1.0 on a low -log10(FDR) (~5). Most sense codons cluster within +/-0.3 of zero.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM P-site codon volcano (same axes and colour key). The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+1.7; among sense codons the largest enrichments sit to the right (TTA, ACG, CCG near +0.3 to +0.4) and the largest depletions to the left (GGA, AAG near -0.2 to -0.3), most pinned near the top of the panel.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM E-site codon volcano. The in-frame stop TGA (grey) is the far-right outlier at log2(OR) ~+2.1; the proline codon CCA (neutral orange) is the largest sense depletion at the far left (~-0.5); sense enrichments (ACG, TCG, CGG, GGG; orange/purple/blue/orange) cluster to the right within ~+0.3 to +0.4.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control A-site codon volcano, the densest panel. A band of depleted codons (AAC, AAG, ACC, CAA, GCC; purple/blue/green) is pinned at the top-left and a matching band of enrichments (CCA, GCG, CTG, CCG, CGG, CGA; orange/green/blue) at the top-right, all within ~+/-0.6; the in-frame stop TGA (grey) sits alone at the far left (~-1.2) on a low -log10(FDR).](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control P-site codon volcano. Enrichments (CCA, CCG, CGA, CGG, AGG; orange/blue) spread to the right (up to ~+1) and depletions (AAG, ACC, CTT, CAC; blue/green/purple) to the left, both pinned near the top; the grey TGA point sits near the origin at low -log10(FDR), non-significant in control.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control E-site codon volcano. Enrichments (CTG, CCG, CGA, CGG, GCG; green/orange/blue) extend to the right (up to ~+0.5) and depletions (GGA, CCA, AAG; orange/blue) to the left, near the top of the panel; the in-frame stop TGA (grey) sits at the far left (~-0.6) on a low -log10(FDR), non-significant in control.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/codon/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
